data.description_suggestion: Reverts a previously authorized token payment hold for the specified transaction. This operation cancels the authorization without processing the payment, releasing the held funds back to the customer. Requires the transaction ID and HMAC authentication. Returns confirmation of the reverted authorization. Use this when a token payment authorization needs to be cancelled before settlement.
data.reasoning: Operation has no description and vague summary. Added clarity about what 'revert' means in payment context (cancels authorization, releases funds), specified the transaction ID requirement, mentioned authentication, clarified return value, and provided context for when to use this operation to improve AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Request a payment report via callback

data.description_suggestion: Processes a payment charge using a previously tokenized card, initiated by the merchant without customer interaction (MIT - Merchant-Initiated Transaction). Accepts a TokenPaymentRequest with the stored token, amount, and transaction details. Returns a TokenPaymentResponse with the transaction result. MIT charges do not require 3D Secure authentication and are suitable for recurring billing, subscription renewals, and one-click purchases. Requires HMAC authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary lacked clarity about the transaction type and use case. data.summary_suggestion: Create authorization hold on stored card (MIT)
data.description_suggestion: Creates an authorization hold on a previously tokenized card, initiated by the merchant without customer interaction (MIT - Merchant-Initiated Transaction). Accepts a TokenPaymentRequest with the stored token, hold amount, and transaction details. Returns a TokenPaymentResponse with the authorization hold result and transactionId. The hold reserves funds but does not capture them; use commitTokenAuthorizationHold to complete the transaction. MIT holds do not require 3D Secure authentication. Requires HMAC authentication.

data.description_suggestion: Processes a payment charge using a previously tokenized card, initiated by the customer (CIT - Customer-Initiated Transaction). Suitable for customer-initiated purchases and account top-ups. Requires HMAC authentication.

Description was missing, leaving AI agents unable to understand: the CIT context (customer-initiated), the hold mechanism, the follow-up operation (commitTokenAuthorizationHold), conditional 3DS requirements, response structure, and authentication. Added explanation of CIT authorization holds and their relationship to the commit operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Create authorization hold on stored card (CIT)
data.description_suggestion: Creates an authorization hold on a previously tokenized card, initiated by the customer (CIT - Customer-Initiated Transaction). Accepts a TokenPaymentRequest with the stored token, hold amount, and transaction details. Returns a TokenPaymentResponse with the authorization hold result and transactionId. The hold reserves funds but does not capture them; use commitTokenAuthorizationHold to complete the transaction. CIT holds may require 3D Secure authentication depending on card issuer rules; the response may include a ThreeDSecureResponse with authentication requirements. Requires HMAC authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary mentioned 3DS but lacked CIT context. Description was missing, leaving AI agents unable to understand: the CIT context (customer-initiated), the hold mechanism, the follow-up operation (commitTokenAuthorizationHold), conditional 3DS requirements, response structure, and authentication. Added explanation of CIT authorization holds and their relationship to the commit operation.

message: Operation 'commitTokenAuthorizationHold' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Summary was vague about the commit operation's purpose and relationship to authorization holds. Description was missing, preventing AI agents from understanding: the prerequisite (prior authorization hold), the transactionId requirement, the capture mechanics (amount constraints), the two-step workflow, request/response schemas, and authentication. Added clarity about the authorization-capture workflow and amount validation.

data.description_suggestion: Completes a previously created authorization hold by capturing the reserved funds. Requires the transactionId from the initial authorization hold operation (createMitAuthorizationHold or createCitAuthorizationHold) and a TokenPaymentRequest with the capture amount and transaction details. Returns a TokenPaymentResponse with the capture result. The capture amount must not exceed the original hold amount. This operation finalizes the two-step payment process (authorize then capture). Requires HMAC authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary was vague about the commit operation's purpose and relationship to authorization holds. Description was missing, preventing AI agents from understanding: the prerequisite (prior authorization hold), the transactionId requirement, the capture mechanics (amount constraints), the two-step workflow, request/response schemas, and authentication. Added clarity about the authorization-capture workflow and amount validation.
